Welcome.title: Welcome to Energy Dashboard!
Welcome.description: To start viewing your electricity consumption you need to connect your Datadis account, the official platform of Spanish electricity distributors.
Welcome.step1Body1: If you don't have an account yet, register at datadis.es with your NIF and your electricity contract details. Account validation usually takes 24–48 h.
Welcome.step1Body2: If you already have an active account, go straight to step 2.
Welcome.step2Body1: In the Settings page enter your NIF and Datadis password. Your password is stored encrypted in the database and is never sent to the browser.
Welcome.step2Body2: Then click Verify connection to confirm everything works and select your supply point (CUPS).
Welcome.step3Body1: Use the Sync button to import up to 2 years of history. After that, synchronisation runs automatically every night at 05:00.
Welcome.step3Note: Datadis publishes data with a delay of up to 2 days.
Welcome.securityBody: Your Datadis credentials never leave the server. Datadis API calls are always made from the backend, never from the browser. Your password is stored encrypted with AES-256-GCM.

Help.title: User guide
Help.subtitle: Everything you need to know to set up and get the most out of the dashboard.
Help.whatIsBody1: Datadis (datadis.es) is the official platform of Spanish electricity distributors. It centralises data from your smart meter: hourly consumption, maximeter and supply data.
Help.whatIsBody2: To register you need your NIF and your electricity contract details (CUPS or invoice reference). Account activation usually takes 24–48 h.
Help.credentialsBody1: Go to Settings and enter your NIF and Datadis password. Click Verify connection to confirm they are correct. If you have multiple supply points (CUPS), they will be listed and you can select the ones you want to monitor.
Help.credentialsBody2: If you have authorised access to another person's meter (e.g. a family member), enter their NIF in the Authorised NIF field.
Help.securityBody: Your Datadis password is stored encrypted in the database with AES-256-GCM. It is never sent to the browser or logged. All Datadis API calls are made exclusively from the server.
Help.syncBody1: Automatic synchronisation runs every night at 05:00. You can also trigger it manually from the Settings page or using the button in the top bar.
Help.syncBody2: Datadis publishes data with a delay of up to 2 days, so the latest available records are usually from the day before yesterday.
Help.syncBody3: On the first sync, up to 2 years of history are imported.
Help.tariffIntro: The standard Spanish domestic tariff (2.0TD) divides the day into three periods with different energy prices:
Help.p1Desc: Monday to Friday from 10:00 to 14:00 and from 18:00 to 22:00. Most expensive.
Help.p2Desc: Monday to Friday from 08:00 to 10:00, from 14:00 to 18:00 and from 22:00 to 00:00. Mid-price.
Help.p3Desc: Nights (00:00–08:00), weekends and national holidays. Cheapest.
Help.tariffNote: National holidays are treated entirely as P3. If you have a PVPC tariff, the price varies hour by hour according to the market.
Help.summaryDesc: Current month consumption, estimated cost, real-time PVPC price and recommendations for the cheapest hours for appliances.
Help.consumptionDesc: Hourly, daily and monthly charts. Comparison with the same time window of previous weeks and breakdown by P1/P2/P3 period.
Help.costDesc: Bill estimate based on your configured tariff. You can simulate different prices to compare commercial options.
Help.pvpcDesc: Comparison between your real consumption and the market price (REData). Useful for evaluating whether PVPC suits you or if a fixed rate would be better.
Help.faq1A: Check that the CUPS is selected in Settings and that the Datadis account has available history. Remember that data arrives with up to 2 days delay.
Help.faq2A: Yes. If your Datadis account has access to multiple CUPS (e.g. home and garage), they will all appear when you verify the connection. Activate the ones you want to monitor. Use the supply selector at the top of the dashboard to switch between them.
Help.faq3Q: The estimated cost doesn't match my bill
Help.faq3A: The cost shown by the dashboard is an estimate based only on energy consumed. The actual bill also includes the power term, taxes (VAT, electricity tax) and other charges that depend on your retailer.
Help.faq4Q: How do I enable price notifications?
Help.faq4A: In Settings → Price notifications, set a threshold in €/kWh and enable push notifications. The browser will ask for permission the first time.

Home.chargeNote: Cheapest average-price window for each appliance

Settings.name: Name
Settings.authorizedNifPlaceholder: Only if you are not the contract holder
Settings.contractedPowerNote: (optional — to calculate the power term in the bill)
Settings.monthlyTargetNote: (optional — alerts if you are about to exceed the target)
Settings.monthViewModeNote: (how the month is calculated in the summary)
Settings.notificationDesc: Notification when the price drops below this value.
Settings.browserNotSupported: This browser does not support notifications
Settings.vapidNotConfigured: VAPID keys not configured on the server
Settings.validationPriceRequired: Enter P1, P2 and P3 prices for the fixed tariff
Settings.validationPricePositive: Prices must be greater than zero
Settings.validationCupsFormat: CUPS must start with ES and be 20–22 characters long
Settings.datadisNote: Datadis can have up to 2 days delay and limits to ~1 request/day per endpoint. If you see error 429, wait 24h. The automatic cron runs at 05:00.
Settings.historyLoadNote: Fetches consumption from Datadis and PVPC prices from REData for the selected period. Datadis has a daily request quota — if it hits the limit, the months already fetched are saved and PVPC prices are synced regardless.
Settings.deleteDataDesc: Permanently delete all your consumption history, supplies and account. This action cannot be undone.
Settings.deleteDataConfirm: Are you sure? This will delete all your consumption history and your account. This cannot be undone.

Cost.noPowerNote: Configure contracted power in Settings to include the power term.
Cost.projectionNote: Based on average of {days} days elapsed
Cost.pvpcCoverageNote: PVPC price coverage of {pct}%. kWh without PVPC price are not included in the market calculation.
Cost.exceededWarning: The maximum recorded power exceeds the contracted power. Depending on your retailer, this may generate penalties. Consider increasing the contracted power or reducing simultaneous appliance use.
Cost.powerDowngrade: You could reduce your contracted power
Cost.powerSafeMargin: Suggested power includes a 20% safety margin above your 3-month peak. Verify the available options with your retailer before making changes.
Cost.powerUpgradeWarning: Your peak demand exceeds the contracted power. This may incur penalties depending on your retailer. Consider increasing your contracted power.

Consumption.subtitlePattern: Average consumption per hour of day
Consumption.subtitleHeatmap: Average consumption by hour and day of week
Consumption.heatmapHover: Hover over a cell to see the average consumption

Pvpc.title: Consumption + PVPC · Last 30 days
Pvpc.bestHoursDesc: Price typically low in the early morning. Ideal for washing machines, dishwashers, EV charging.
Pvpc.avoidHoursDesc: P1 peaks between 10–14h and 18–22h. Shifting appliances to off-peak hours can reduce cost by 30–40%.

Offers.profileSubtitle: Share this data with electricity comparison sites to get personalised quotes.
Offers.profileTip: Use the annual kWh, P1/P2/P3 breakdown and contracted power when requesting quotes from electricity retailers.
Offers.pvpcCompTitle: Your tariff vs PVPC market · {months} months
Offers.pvpcCompSubFixed: Comparison between your fixed rate and what you would have paid at PVPC variable market prices.
Offers.pvpcCompSubPvpc: You are on the PVPC tariff. The table shows your actual costs. Use the tariff simulator below to compare with fixed-rate options.
Offers.pvpcCoverageNote: PVPC price data available for {pct}% of consumption. Months with partial coverage may be approximate.
Offers.externalSubtitle: Official comparison tools to find better electricity deals in Spain.
Offers.cnmcDesc: Official Spanish energy regulator — find the electricity offer comparator on their website

TariffSimulator.title: Tariff change calculator
TariffSimulator.subtitle: Simulate what your consumption would cost with different P1/P2/P3 prices
TariffSimulator.simulatedSaves: The simulated tariff would save {amount} €/year with your current consumption pattern.
TariffSimulator.simulatedCosts: The simulated tariff would cost {amount} €/year more than the current one.
TariffSimulator.enterPrices: Enter the prices of the tariff to compare to see the simulation.
